learning_outcomes.description: Define and explain the term global change and discuss related physical, social, cultural, economic and environmental problems and prospects.
Examine the spatial tools that are utilized in the investigation of global change.
Analyse and explain the physical dimensions associated with global change processes.
Analyse and explain the socio-cultural dimensions associated with global change processes.
Demonstrate insight into the future prospects of global change issues.
Employ verbal and written communication skills effectively to articulate your knowledge.
Reflect on your own learning experiences within the course.
Discuss research findings in the area of global change.

assessments.assessment_title: Reflection and Critical Assessment on a Global Change Dimension, with Local Impact
assessments.task_description:
The primary objectives of this assessment are:

(1) To reflect on our discussions and materials covered in Lectures, Readings and Tutorials in Weeks 1-4 and use this information to select a suitable "Global change action"; and

(2) To plan how to build a detailed understanding of one type of "global change", including its known causes/drivers, impacts and approaches taken to reduce negative impacts of this change.

This is an individual task.

For this assessment item use the following headings in a structured document, up to 2500 words, (include figures, tables and references if helpful).

References are not included in the word limit:

Headings:

i) Title page

ii) Table of Contents

iii) Declaration of AI Generated Content using UQ format declarations - https://guides.library.uq.edu.au/referencing/acknowledging-and-referencing-ai

  1. Selected Global Change Dimension
  2. Reflection on Tutorial Discussions - In Context of Global Change Dimensions
  3. Summary and Critical Assessment of Relevant Literature (+ 5-10 papers)
  4. References

Refer to Assessment Section of the course Blackboard page, for detailed instructions, submission template and grading criteria

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date and time. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Recorded Presentation on National to Local Scale Adaptation to Global Change
assessments.task_description:
This project is an investigation into a "Global Change and Solution" to set out how you will build a detailed understanding of it, and how it is being addressed at local, nation-state or global scales. 

The primary objectives of this assessment are:

To build a detailed understanding and preliminary assessment of one type of "global change", including its known causes/drivers, impacts and approaches taken to reduce negative impacts of this change at local scales at a location in Australia

Task:

Integrate the practical skills and knowledge of global change you have developed in the course this assessment item requires you to prepare a recorded presentation providing a 5-minute long "project briefing" video.

This is an individual task.

Refer to Assessment Section of the course Blackboard page, for detailed instructions, submission template and grading criteria

In the Briefing Video you outline to an audience from the general public, a specific type of global change, along with examples of its impacts and management at a local scale in Australia, and a brief assessment of how effective its management has been.

Your aim is to use the brief to convince the audience that:

-     the global change dimension you assess is established based on credible scientific evidence;

-     the local scale impacts of the global change dimension you assess are identifiable and can be attributed to global scale changes;

-     specific management actions have been taken by a form of government or community group to adapt to, and/or mitigate the local scale impacts; and

-     that you have evidence from a critical assessment of the management measures if they are reducing impacts and risk from the global scale change.

 All instructions for completing and submitting this assessment item, including an example presentation, are on the course Blackboard site.

 Media to Use:

Your recording can be compiled using PowerPoint, Prezi, or any other software. 

The presentation will need to be based on material covered during the semester, along with significant amount of background research in peer-reviewed literature.

The presentation will need to be based primarily on relevant graphic (images, maps, graphs, photos, diagrams), with limited use of detailed text, e.g. less than 30% of each slide.

 Options for recording your presentation – this must include you speaking to camera and your visuals:

a)   PowerPoints Record function

b)   Zoom recording

c)   Open source or proprietary video recording and editing software e.g. https://obsproject.com/ or other options

 The submitted material, in a single word or PDF document, though the course Blackboard site submission link should include the following:

-      Title page, including your “topic”, course name, student number

-      Contents listing

-      Link to an accessible site where you have uploaded your video, e.g. YouTube

-      Dot-point outline of your presentation, e.g. headings and dot points

-      Reference list including all materials used to generate the content and presentation

 The recording is not to be sped up to meet the 5-minute limit - modified videos will be deducted grades for this.

Permissible Use of Generative AI Content in Assessment Item 

- This Assessment tasks allows use of AI to research and design the presentation. AI cannot be used to deliver the presentation.

All uses of AI tools must be fully documented and acknowledged to identify which parts of the assessment were produced using AI tools.

This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge, ideally without the aid of Artificial Intelligence (AI).

Students are advised that the use of AI technologies beyond the limits set here, is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

Some advice: Refer to the Student Support website and the Graphic Presentation tutorial for information on the presentation of your assessment item. Please consult Student Services website (www.uq.edu.au/student ) for workshops and general advice on essay writing. The Library also offers workshops in the use of EndNote bibliographic referencing software.

assessments.task_description:
Understanding and Working with Global Changes - Critical assessment of tutorial discussion includes presentation notes.

The primary objectives of this assessment are:

An integrated assessment of all course elements - in the form of 3-5 short-form essays to be completed as part of a central examination.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
